name: Temple of Hephaestus
description: Single-clue route at one of the best-preserved ancient Doric temples in Greece.

quiz.question: This 5th-century BC Doric temple stands in the Ancient Agora and is dedicated to the god of metalworking and craftsmanship. Its columns and frieze are remarkably intact. Which temple is it?

name: Hadrian's Arch
description: Single-clue route at the Roman gate marking the boundary between old and new Athens.

quiz.question: This Roman triumphal arch was built in 131 AD. Inscriptions on either side still read 'This is Athens, the ancient city of Theseus' and 'This is the city of Hadrian'. Which monument marks the divide between old and new Athens?

name: Tower of the Winds
description: Single-clue route at the ancient octagonal clocktower in the Roman Agora.

quiz.question: In the Roman Agora stands an octagonal marble tower from the 1st century BC. It once combined a water clock, sundials, and a wind vane. Greeks often call it 'Aerides'. What is its common English name?

name: Stoa of Attalos
description: Single-clue route at the reconstructed Hellenistic portico housing the Agora Museum.

quiz.question: This two-storey colonnade was a gift from a king of Pergamon. It was fully reconstructed in the 1950s and now houses the Museum of the Ancient Agora. Which stoa is it?

name: Zappeion
description: Single-clue route at the neoclassical exhibition hall built for the revival of the Olympic Games.

quiz.question: This neoclassical building was donated by the Zappas family to promote the revival of the Olympic Games. It stands in the National Garden and now hosts exhibitions and summits. What is it called?

name: Old Royal Palace – Parliament
description: Single-clue route at the neoclassical building that became the Greek Parliament.

quiz.question: This neoclassical palace was built in 1836–1843 as the royal residence. Today it houses the Hellenic Parliament and the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier stands before it. Which building is it?

name: Lysikrates Monument
description: Single-clue route at the first external use of Corinthian columns in Athens.

quiz.question: This circular monument in Plaka was built by a choregos to support the tripod he won in a drama contest. It is the first monument in Athens to use Corinthian columns on the outside. Which is it?

name: Odeon of Herodes Atticus
description: Single-clue route at the Roman stone theatre on the south slope of the Acropolis.

quiz.question: This Roman stone theatre was built in 161 AD by Herodes Atticus in memory of his wife. It sits on the south slope of the Acropolis and still hosts summer performances. What is it called?
